title: Oora Therinjikitten Lyrics Padikkathavan(1985)
stanzas:
yaedhu bandha paasam
ellaam veli vesam
kaasu panam vandhaal
nesam sila maasam

enveettu kannukutti
ennodu mallukatti
emmaarbil muttudhadi
kanmani en kanmani.

thee patta kaayathula
thael vandhu kottuthadi
kanmani en kanmani ..

naettru ivan yaeni
indru ivan niyaani
aala kara serthu
aadum indha thoni

sondhame oru vaanavil
andha varnam konja neram.
bandhame mullaanadhaal
indha nenjil oru baaram

panangaasa kanduputtaa
puli kooda pulla thinnum
kanmani en kanmani

adangaadha kaala onnu
adi maadaa ponadhadi
kanmani en kanmani..

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song 'Oora Therinjikitten'?

The song expresses a sense of realization and joy about life and love, reflecting the emotions of the protagonist.

What is the context of this song in the movie 'Padikkathavan'?

In Padikkathavan, this song highlights the protagonist's journey of self-discovery and his feelings towards love.

Who is the singer and music director of this song?

The song is sung by K.J. Yesudhas and composed by Ilaiyaraaja.

When was 'Oora Therinjikitten' released?

The song was released in 1985.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In 'Oora Therinjikitten', the protagonist expresses a newfound understanding of love and life, evoking feelings of nostalgia and happiness. The song's melodic structure, combined with K.J. Yesudhas's soulful voice, creates an emotional connection with the audience. It serves as a celebration of life's simple joys and the beauty of relationships. The lyrics beautifully encapsulate the essence of realization and affection, making it a memorable piece from the film.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Oora Therinjikitten from the classic Padikkathavan is a timeless melody that resonates with nostalgia and joy. The enchanting vocals of K.J. Yesudhas, paired with Ilaiyaraaja's masterful composition, create an unforgettable listening experience. Dive into this beautiful song that captures the essence of love and life in its lyrics.
stanzaMeanings: I have understood the town, I have understood the world, my dear, my dear.
Knowledge has been born, I have understood it every day, my dear, my dear.
I raised a green child, fed it milk, and it has become a snake.
Whatever bond of love comes, it is all a disguise; when money comes, love lasts for a few months.
I thought about blood, I thought about whether all this is worth it.
The betel leaf is bitter, who is it for?
My little girl at home, my little girl, is like a pearl in my mother's lap.
In the burnt fruit, oil has come and hit me, my dear, my dear.
Yesterday he was like me, today he is a wise man; this boat dances together.
There is a rainbow in the sky, that color lasts for a little while. The thorny flower in my heart is a burden.
I found a tiger in the grass, even the tiger eats grass, my dear, my dear.
The time that doesn't stop, it hits hard, my dear, my dear.
I have understood the town, I have understood the world, my dear, my dear.
Knowledge has been born, I have understood it every day, my dear, my dear.
I raised a green child, fed it milk, and it has become a snake.
